original: NASA’s trail-blazing Ingenuity helicopter, the first aircraft to fly on another world, dies after three years on Mars and 72 flights (only expected to make five flights)
Brazilian surfer Maya Gabeira rides the largest-ever wave by a female surfer at 20.72 m (68 ft) at Praia do Norte in Nazaréin, Portugal
First drone rescue of swimmers by lifeguards in New South Wales, Australia
Perplexed Pilgrims in Boston reported America's 1st UFO sighting
1st demonstration of an X-ray machine in US (NYC)
South African batsman AB de Villiers makes fastest century in ODI cricket history from 31 balls during an innings of 149 against the West Indies in Johannesburg
Phil Smith and Phil Mayfield become the first two people to BASE jump from objects in all four categories (buildings, cliffs, bridges, antennae) after parachuting off a Houston skyscraper
A 611 gram chondrite type meteorite stikes a house near the village of Baxter in Stone County, Missouri
Fragments from the Tagish Lake meteorite fall to Earth in northwestern British Columbia
Astronomers discover pulsar in remnants of Supernova 1987A (LMC)
The world's largest commercial jet, the Airbus A380, is unveiled in France
US city of Lexington starts a new tourism campaign aimed at extraterrestrial travelers by beaming a welcome message into space via an infrared laser
Boerge Ousland of Norway becomes the first person to cross Antarctica alone and unaided
IOC restoresJim Thorpe's Olympic medals (Pentathlon & Decathlon victories) 70 years after they were taken from him for being paid $25 in semi-pro baseball
Willie O’Ree is the 1st African-American to appear in the NHL, making his debut for the Boston Bruins in a 3-0 victory in Montreal
1st polar bear exhibited in America in Boston
The Cando event, a possible bolide impact in Cando, Spain. Witnesses claim to have seen a fireball in the sky lasting for almost one minute.
The Beatles make their first appearance on US Billboard Chart with single "I Want to Hold Your Hand" at #45
Raymond Briggs, British children's book author and illustrator (The Snowman; Fungus the Bogeyman), born in Wimbledon, London, England (d. 2022)
NASA and NOAA announce that 2016 was the hottest year on record, surpassing the previous record set in 2015 which itself topped a record set in 2014
Microsoft says it is buying Activision Blizzard, publisher of online games "Call of Duty", "World of Warcraft" and "Candy Crush" for $70 billion - biggest-ever gaming and tech takeover
Stop Online Piracy Act (SOPA) blackout becomes the largest protest in the history of the internet
NY Lotto pays $30.5 million to one winner (#s are 19-20-27-34-41-46)
Scientists identify a previously unknown bacterium as the cause of the mysterious Legionnaires' disease.
John Cade, Australian psychiatrist, discovered the psychiatric benefits of lithium in 1949, born in Murtoa, Australia (d. 1980)

Hurricane Kyrill: kills at least 44 in Western Europe: strongest British storm in 17 years kills 14 people, worst German storm since 1999 with 13 deaths and causes container Ship MSC Napoli to be destroyed off coast of Devon
Mount Lamington in Papua New Guinea erupts, killing 2,942 people
1st shipboard landing of a plane (Tanforan Park to USS Pennsylvania)
The first transatlantic radio transmission to originate in the United States is sent by a transmitter in Massachusetts
Thiokol conducts 2nd test firing of space shuttle's SRB

First college basketball game with 5 players on each side is conducted by the University of Iowa; invites student athletes from University of Chicago for an experimental game; Chicago beats Iowa 15-12
Super Bowl X, Miami Orange Bowl, Miami, FL: Pittsburgh Steelers beat Dallas Cowboys, 21-17; MVP: Lynn Swann, Pittsburgh, WR
Martin Luther King Jr.holiday observed in all 50 states of the USA for 1st time
Boston Bruins retire Willie O'Ree's #22 jersey, on the 64th anniversary of his becoming the NHL's first Black player
CaptainJames Cookstumbles over the Sandwich Islands (Hawaiian Islands)
Argentine generalJosé de San Martínleads a revolutionary army over the Andes to attack Spanish royalists in Chile
King Naresuan of Siam kills Crown Prince Minchit Sra of Burma in single combat, date is now observed as Royal Thai Armed Forces day
Robert C. Weaver, confirmed as 1st Black US presidential cabinet member (Housing and Urban Development - HUD)
Thomas A. Watson, American inventor, assistant to Alexander Graham Bell in inventing the telephone, born in Salem, Massachusetts (d. 1934)[1]
Edward Frankland, British structural chemist who co-discovered helium and developed the theory of valence, born in Lancaster, England (d. 1899)
Arthur Ransome, English author, journalist and critic (Swallows & Amazons), born in Leeds, England (d. 1967)
John Cleese's final episode on "Monty Python's Flying Circus" on BBC TV
Danny Kaye [David Daniel Kaminski], American comedian and actor (Danny Kaye Show, White Christmas), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1987)
Frankie Knuckles [Francis Warren Nicholls, Jr.], American DJ and record producer who popularized house music in Chicago during the 1980s, born in The Bronx, New York (d. 2014)
Gilles Villeneuve, Canadian auto racer (World F1 runner-up 1979; Ferrari), born in Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu, Quebec (d. 1982)
David Ruffin, American soul musician and early lead singer for The Temptations ("My Girl"; "Ain't Too Proud to Beg"), born in Whynot, Mississippi (d. 1991)
Peter Jenkins finishes "A Walk Across America" at Florence, Oregon
A series of atmospheric river storms pummelling California finally subside, after more than 500 mudslides with "extensive" damage to 40 out of 58 counties, killing 20 people
White Sands National Monument in New Mexico established
Modern field hockey is born with the formation of The Hockey Association in England
24th Space Shuttle (61-C) Mission-Columbia 7-returns to Earth
Joseph Glidden, American inventor of 1st commercial usable barbed wire, born in Charlestown, New Hampshire (d. 1906)
Jacob Bronowski, Polish-born British mathematician and science writer best known as the presenter of the BBC television series, The Ascent of Man, born in Łódź, Poland (d. 1974)
Yoichiro Nambu, Japanese-born American Physicist (Nobel Prize 2008), born in Tokyo (d. 2015)
David Bellamy, English botanist & TV presenter, born in London, England (d. 2019)
John Hume, Northern Irish politician (jointly awarded Nobel Peace Prize for Good Friday Agreement), born in Derry, Northern Ireland (d. 2020)[1]
Tom Bailey, English rock vocalist, keyboardist (Thompson Twins - "Hold Me Now", "Doctor! Doctor!"), and record producer, born in Halifax, West Yorkshire
Jeffrey N Williams, United States Army officer & NASA astronaut, born in Village of Superior, Wisconsin
Mark Rylance [David Mark Rylance Waters], English actor (Bridge of Spies) & theatre director, born in Ashford, Kent
Virgil Hill, American boxer (WBA light heavyweight title 1987, 97; IBF & lineal light heavyweight titles 1996-97; WBA cruiserweight title 2000-02, 2006-07), born in Clinton, Missouri
